blocks.content: Nathan found the fire pit the way Nathan found most things — by heading toward whatever had the most light.
Maya was already there.

She was sitting on a piece of driftwood at the edge of the ring, shoes off, feet in the sand, holding a glass of something and looking at the water. She had the look of someone who had been watching a room full of people be happy and had quietly started feeling something adjacent to that herself — not quite there yet, but close. Closer than she'd expected.
Nathan sat beside her without asking. He'd learned that asking gave her an opportunity to say no that she didn't actually want.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: Of all the places to sit.

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: This is objectively the best seat.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: It's a piece of driftwood.

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: Ocean view. Fire. Excellent company. That's five stars.
She looked at him.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: You counted yourself as part of the rating.

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: I included myself generously, yes.
A small laugh escaped her. Not the polite kind she gave most people — the shorter, real one, the one that came out when something surprised her.
Nathan heard it and didn't make anything of it. He just looked at the fire.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: It was a good ceremony.

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: It really was.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: She looked — I don't know. She looked like herself. The whole self. Not the version that's managing something.
She said it quietly, like she hadn't meant it to come out that large.

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: Yeah.

He said it simply. He understood what she meant — had watched Cassandra walk down that aisle and seen the same thing Maya was describing, the specific quality of someone who has put down a weight and knows they don't have to pick it back up.

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: Ethan has that effect.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: I know.
A pause. The fire moved. The ocean said what it always said.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: I wasn't sure about him at first.

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: He wasn't sure about him at first either.
She looked at Nathan. He looked back, entirely undefensive.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: That's actually reassuring.

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: I have a talent for honesty at low personal cost.
